input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

### 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime
`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. Switch to **`DynamicResource`** so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when you swap the ResourceDictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code by merging the new dictionary:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

### 2. Android restarts the Activity on UI-mode change
By default Android **recreates** the Activity when the system dark mode toggles (it's a `uiMode` configuration change). In your `Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself, preventing the restart. You can then detect the switch and apply your theme:

```csharp
// e.g. in App.xaml.cs or a service
public static void ApplySystemTheme()
{
    var mode = Application.Current.RequestedTheme; // Light or Dark
    var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
    mergedDicts.Clear();
    mergedDicts.Add(mode == AppTheme.Dark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
}
```

Hook it up with `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) => ApplySystemTheme();`.

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource`, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your `MainActivity`.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app regarding dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes the Android system dark mode setting, the app restarts (Activity gets recreated). This is because Android recreates the Activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. They need to handle this in the Android manifest or in their Activity configuration.


I'll provide a straightforward solution addressing both problems: switching to `DynamicResource` for theme-aware color bindings, and configuring the Android manifest to prevent Activity recreation on UI mode changes by adding `uiMode` to the `configChanges` attribute.
